title: dactylorhiza caucasica var. markowitschii
intro: Dactylorhiza caucasica var. markowitschii, also known as Markowitz's orchid or Caucasian marsh orchid, is a rare and endemic plant species native to the Caucasus region. This terrestrial orchid is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza caucasica var. markowitschii include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to treat skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
